trans-unit.source: Exercise 2: Disaster impact in different regions of Senegal
trans-unit.target: Exercise 2: Disaster impact in different regions of Senegal

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}

trans-unit.source: Aim of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Aim of the exercise

trans-unit.source: Become familiar with different types of non-spatial analysis and geoprocessing tools. Understand the process of discovering relationships and connections between features in spatial data.
trans-unit.target: Become familiar with different types of non-spatial analysis and geoprocessing tools. Understand the process of discovering relationships and connections between features in spatial data.

trans-unit.source: Type of trainings exercise:
trans-unit.target: Type of trainings exercise:

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: Focus group (GIS-Knowledge Level)
trans-unit.target: Focus group (GIS-Knowledge Level)

trans-unit.source: These skills are relevant for
trans-unit.target: These skills are relevant for

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}

trans-unit.source: Estimated time demand for the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Estimated time demand for the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Relevant Wiki Articles
trans-unit.target: Relevant Wiki Articles

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Spatial Queries](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Spatial Queries](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Categorized classification](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Categorized classification](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Availbale Data
trans-unit.target: Availbale Data

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :link: https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_5/Modul_5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal/Modul5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal.zip __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_5/Modul_5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal/Modul5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal.zip) and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__ :::
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :link: https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_5/Modul_5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal/Modul5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal.zip __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_5/Modul_5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal/Modul5_Exercise1_Disaster_effects_in_different_regions_Senegal.zip) and save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__ :::

trans-unit.source: `sen_admbnda_adm1_1m_gov_ocha_20190426.shp`: [Senegal boundary data (Admin level 1)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/senegal-administrative-boundaries)
trans-unit.target: `sen_admbnda_adm1_1m_gov_ocha_20190426.shp`: [Senegal boundary data (Admin level 1)](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/senegal-administrative-boundaries)

trans-unit.source: `DI_Stat924.xls`: [Senegal Desinventar Sendai data](https://www.desinventar.net/DesInventar/profiletab.jsp?countrycode=sen) showing effects of disasters in Senegalese regions
trans-unit.target: `DI_Stat924.xls`: [Senegal Desinventar Sendai data](https://www.desinventar.net/DesInventar/profiletab.jsp?countrycode=sen) showing effects of disasters in Senegalese regions

trans-unit.source: `sen_admpop_adm1_2020.csv`: Senegal administrative level 1 (région) 2019 [projection population statistics](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/senegal-population-statistics)
trans-unit.target: `sen_admpop_adm1_2020.csv`: Senegal administrative level 1 (région) 2019 [projection population statistics](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/senegal-population-statistics)

trans-unit.source: Task
trans-unit.target: Task

trans-unit.source: Create an overview of the effects of disasters in different regions of Senegal. Use non-spatial joins, table functions and different symbology.
trans-unit.target: Create an overview of the effects of disasters in different regions of Senegal. Use non-spatial joins, table functions and different symbology.

trans-unit.source: Load the Senegal administrative boundary layer (`sen_admbnda_adm1_1m_gov_ocha_20190426.shp`), as well as population per subnational unit (`sen_admpop_adm1_2020.csv`) and the Desinventar Sendai data of Senegal (`DI_Stat924.xls`) into QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Load the Senegal administrative boundary layer (`sen_admbnda_adm1_1m_gov_ocha_20190426.shp`), as well as population per subnational unit (`sen_admpop_adm1_2020.csv`) and the Desinventar Sendai data of Senegal (`DI_Stat924.xls`) into QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Make sure to reproject the dataset with the administrative boundaries into UTM zone 28N. See the [wiki entry on Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Make sure to reproject the dataset with the administrative boundaries into UTM zone 28N. See the [wiki entry on Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Conduct non-spatial joins based on regions listed in two datasets and the PCODE listed in these same sets. See the [Wiki entry on Non-spatial joins](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Conduct non-spatial joins based on regions listed in two datasets and the PCODE listed in these same sets. See the [Wiki entry on Non-spatial joins](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_non_spatial_joins_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the different attribute tables with the corresponding columns highlighted :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the different attribute tables with the corresponding columns highlighted :::

trans-unit.source: First, add the total population of each administrative area to the shapefiles. Select the correct column that should be added (Hint: search for the column named `Total`).
trans-unit.target: First, add the total population of each administrative area to the shapefiles. Select the correct column that should be added (Hint: search for the column named `Total`).

trans-unit.source: Then, add the number of directly and indirectly affected people. Also select the correct columns that should be added (Hint: search for the column names `Directly affected` and `Indirectly Affected`).
trans-unit.target: Then, add the number of directly and indirectly affected people. Also select the correct columns that should be added (Hint: search for the column names `Directly affected` and `Indirectly Affected`).

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot for task 4 :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_Join_field.png
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot for task 4 :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_Join_field.png

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the tool Join Attributes by Field Value for the total population. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the tool Join Attributes by Field Value for the total population. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot for task 5 :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_Join_field_2.PNG
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot for task 5 :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_Join_field_2.PNG

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the tool Join Attributes by Field Value for the directly and indirectly affected people. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the tool Join Attributes by Field Value for the directly and indirectly affected people. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: Use the table functions to calculate the area of each region in square kilometres and the density of the population. See the Wiki entry on [Table functions](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Use the table functions to calculate the area of each region in square kilometres and the density of the population. See the Wiki entry on [Table functions](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Create a new column/field named `"area_sqkm"` using the field calculator. Ensure decimal numbers are used as the field type. For the calculation use the expression: `$area / (1000 * 1000)`
trans-unit.target: Create a new column/field named `"area_sqkm"` using the field calculator. Ensure decimal numbers are used as the field type. For the calculation use the expression: `$area / (1000 * 1000)`

trans-unit.source: Create another column/field named `"pop_per_sqkm"` with decimal numbers as field type. Use the expression: `"Total" / "area_sqkm"` for the calculation.
trans-unit.target: Create another column/field named `"pop_per_sqkm"` with decimal numbers as field type. Use the expression: `"Total" / "area_sqkm"` for the calculation.

trans-unit.source: You can access the field calculator through your attribute table by activating ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle editing mode` and clicking on this symbol ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to `Open field calculator`.
trans-unit.target: You can access the field calculator through your attribute table by activating ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle editing mode` and clicking on this symbol ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to `Open field calculator`.

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot calculation `"area_sqkm"` :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_area_sqkm.png
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot calculation `"area_sqkm"` :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_area_sqkm.png

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the area calculation using the field calculator. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the area calculation using the field calculator. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot calculation `"pop_per_sqkm"` :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_pop_per_sqkm.png
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot calculation `"pop_per_sqkm"` :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_pop_per_sqkm.png

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the population per square km calculation using the field calculator. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the population per square km calculation using the field calculator. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: Now, we need to rename the `Indirectly Affected` and `Directly Affected` columns so they don't contain spaces. This ensures that the field calculator works properly. For this task we will use the tool `Rename field`.
trans-unit.target: Now, we need to rename the `Indirectly Affected` and `Directly Affected` columns so they don't contain spaces. This ensures that the field calculator works properly. For this task we will use the tool `Rename field`.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the Rename field tool. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the Rename field tool. :::

trans-unit.source: Let us now determine the proportion of the population directly or indirectly affected in relation to the total population per region.
trans-unit.target: Let us now determine the proportion of the population directly or indirectly affected in relation to the total population per region.

trans-unit.source: Use the field calculator. Use the expression: `"Indirectly Affected" / ("Total"/100)`
trans-unit.target: Use the field calculator. Use the expression: `"Indirectly Affected" / ("Total"/100)`

trans-unit.source: Use the field calculator. Use the expression: `"Directly Affected" / ("Total"/100)`
trans-unit.target: Use the field calculator. Use the expression: `"Directly Affected" / ("Total"/100)`

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot indirectly affected :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_per_indirect_affected.PNG
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot indirectly affected :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_per_indirect_affected.PNG

trans-unit.source: Calculation of the proportion of the indirectly affected population relative to the total population within each region. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Calculation of the proportion of the indirectly affected population relative to the total population within each region. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot directly affected :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_per_direct_affected.PNG
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Screenshot directly affected :::{figure} /fig/en_ex1_per_direct_affected.PNG

trans-unit.source: Calculation of the proportion of the directly affected population relative to the total population within each region. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Calculation of the proportion of the directly affected population relative to the total population within each region. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: Select a colour scheme using the `Symbology` to visualise the share of people directly affected in the different regions (Hint: `Categorized`).
trans-unit.target: Select a colour scheme using the `Symbology` to visualise the share of people directly affected in the different regions (Hint: `Categorized`).

trans-unit.source: Play around with different modes to find a useful colour/categorisation scheme for the visualisation.
trans-unit.target: Play around with different modes to find a useful colour/categorisation scheme for the visualisation.

trans-unit.source: Which regions are more and which are less directly affected? Are there any data gaps?
trans-unit.target: Which regions are more and which are less directly affected? Are there any data gaps?

trans-unit.source: Export the map as a png (Hint: `Project, Import/Export, Export map to Image`).
trans-unit.target: Export the map as a png (Hint: `Project, Import/Export, Export map to Image`).

trans-unit.source: Repeat the previous steps, but this time visualise the indirectly affected people in each region (Hint: `Categorized`, column `Indirectly affected`).
trans-unit.target: Repeat the previous steps, but this time visualise the indirectly affected people in each region (Hint: `Categorized`, column `Indirectly affected`).

trans-unit.source: What differences can be observed between the regions directly affected and those indirectly affected?
trans-unit.target: What differences can be observed between the regions directly affected and those indirectly affected?
